claim: AI product development requires a bottoms-up, responsive approach rather than traditional top-down planning, because model capabilities are only fully understood late in development
actionable: Shift from 3-6 month waterfall planning to iterative discovery; validate capabilities experimentally rather than assuming feature feasibility upfront
source_title: Anthropic CPO Mike Krieger: Building AI Products From the Bottom Up

claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) emerged from generalizing three similar but independently implemented integrations (Google Drive, GitHub, and a third integration), following the pattern of extracting abstractions after the third implementation
actionable: Implement features three times before investing in abstraction; use consistent patterns to identify reusable protocols
source_title: Anthropic CPO Mike Krieger: Building AI Products From the Bottom Up

claim: Over 70% of Anthropic's internal pull requests now use Claude-generated code, requiring new approaches to code review and architectural oversight
actionable: Plan for AI-assisted code review processes when adoption exceeds 50%; traditional review practices break down at scale
source_title: Anthropic CPO Mike Krieger: Building AI Products From the Bottom Up

claim: Increased engineering velocity from AI coding tools exposes previously tolerable inefficiencies in product alignment meetings, which now waste multiples of the engineering time they displace
actionable: Invest in decision-making processes and product management tooling when adopting fast coding agents; organizational bottlenecks become more painful
source_title: Anthropic CPO Mike Krieger: Building AI Products From the Bottom Up

claim: Younger employees entering the workplace have no stigma using generative AI and expect it as a default tool, while older cohorts had to unlearn initial hesitation
actionable: Design tool training and workflows assuming AI assistance is the default; highlight and normalize AI use in shared channels to break psychological barriers
source_title: Anthropic CPO Mike Krieger: Building AI Products From the Bottom Up

claim: These AI products are still far from intuitive for first-time users; while power users can achieve incredible results, most users need significant guidance on workflows and data flow
actionable: Prioritize onboarding and in-product education over feature breadth; design for guided workflows rather than open-ended capability
source_title: Anthropic CPO Mike Krieger: Building AI Products From the Bottom Up

claim: AI model compute allocation is a critical strategic decision competing with research innovation and product inference; releasing models in the market generates market feedback that improves research direction
actionable: Balance research optimization with shipping products for market signal; witness-driven development captures real use cases missed by pure research optimization
source_title: Anthropic CPO Mike Krieger: Building AI Products From the Bottom Up

claim: AI-native products that only staple models onto existing UIs fail; the application's primitives must be reimagined to position the model as primary, not secondary
actionable: Redesign core product architecture when integrating AI; do not add AI as a sidebar feature to legacy products
source_title: Anthropic CPO Mike Krieger: Building AI Products From the Bottom Up
